CASE MANAGEMENT CONFERENCE
Off Calendar Jul-27-2015 Off Calendar

Notice Of Motion And Motion Of Specially Appearing Movant Monica Pearson To Quash Service On 1) Ground Of Lack Of In Personam Jurisdiction And 2) For Failure To Abide By Code Of Civil Procedure S415.20(B) For Summons And Complaint
Off Calendar May-07-2015 Advanced From Jun-22-15 To May-22-15 At 09:30 A.M. In Dept 302

Notice Of Motion And Motion Of Specially Appearing Movant Monica Pearson To Quash Service On 1) Ground Of Lack Of In Personam Jurisdiction And 2) For Failure To Abide By Code Of Civil Procedure S415.20(B) For Summons And Complaint
Off Calendar May-22-2015 Denied
MINI MINUTES:
Appearances:
Maher Soliman, in pro per, Tel. 415.776.3998, for plaintiffs;
No other appearances in the matter.

Ruling: The matter was not argued. The Court adopts its tentative ruling as follows: DEFENDANT MONICA PEARSON's Motion Of Specially Appearing Movant Monica Pearson To Quash Service On 1) Ground Of Lack Of In Personam Jurisdiction And 2) For Failure To Abide By Code Of Civil Procedure S415.20(B) For Summons And Complaint is denied. A nonresident defendant may be subject to jurisdiction on claims related to its activities in California. Such "limited" or "specific" personal jurisdiction requires a showing that: (1) defendant purposefully established contacts with the forum state; (2) plaintiff's cause of action "arises out of" or is "related to " defendant's contacts with the forum state; and (3) The forum's exercise of personal jurisdiction in the particular case comports with "fair play and substantial justice." [Burger King Corp. v. Rudzewicz (1985) 471 US 462, 477-478, 105 S.Ct. 2174, 2184-2185; see Data Disc, Inc. v. Systems Technology Associates, Inc. (9th Cir. 1977) 557 F2d 1280, 1287; and Vons Cos., Inc. v. Seabest Foods, Inc. (1996) 14 C4th 434, 446, 58 CR2d 899, 906.] Here, Defendant has such contacts with California, and Plaintiff's cause of action arises out of Defendant's contacts. Thus, California may exercise personal jurisdiction over Defendant. Plaintiff must show valid service within the statutory period. [Dill v. Berquist Const. Co., Inc. (1994) 24 CA4th 1426, 1439.] Plaintiff must demonstrate by a preponderance of the evidence that all jurisdictional criteria are met. [Ziller Electronics Lab GmbH v. Sup.Ct. (Grosh Scenic Studios) (1988) 206 CA3d 1222, 1232.] Here, Plaintiff has presented an affidavit providing that Defendant was served with summons within the statutory period. Thus Plaintiff has met his burden. Order signed in open court. Judge Ernest H. Goldsmith; Clerk Felicia Green; Court Reporter Melanie Gheno, CSR No. 7489; Dept. 302. (302/EHG)
